speaker: alice
text: Right, environmental good news first up - and this one's a ripper. <cite index='8-11,9-11'>Green turtles have made such a comeback that they've been downgraded from endangered to least concern on the conservation red list</cite>. After being hunted nearly to extinction for turtle soup in the 1970s, they're now thriving.

speaker: charlie
text: <cite index='8-13,9-13'>It's all thanks to protecting eggs, releasing hatchlings on beaches, and reducing capture in fishing nets</cite>. Shows what we can achieve when we actually put in the effort, doesn't it?

speaker: alice
text: Absolutely, and here's another win - <cite index='4-14'>Portugal generated 80.7 percent of its electricity from renewable energy in January</cite>. That's their best record in nine months and puts them second in Europe overall. The shift to renewables really is unstoppable when countries commit to it.
